_comment: SCA review (src/ + xpi/, the XPI's first-party code minified so SCA is required). unused-permission is input:xpi, so it judges the SHIPPED build (xpi/background.js), not the readable source. The minified build reads relatedMessageId (messagesRead) and displayedFolder (accountsRead) as bare property tokens found by the raw scan -> both escalate and are re-judged PER OCCURRENCE by the shipped-package summary pass (messagesRead#1 pass -> dropped; accountsRead#1 unsure -> manual). compose's dotted injection token never appears in the shipped build, and the scan is decidable (minified but NOT obfuscated, no parse/limitation/dynamic gap), so compose is a DETERMINISTIC unused-permission finding with no LLM - the win of judging the shipped XPI directly. Covers: input:xpi permission scan in SCA, the shipped-package recheck pass, per-occurrence aggregation, and the deterministic-on-XPI path.
